text: 4120 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَرْهَنُ جَارِيَتَهُ أَ يَحِلُّ لَهُ أَنْ يَطَأَهَا قَالَ "إِنَّ اَلَّذِينَ اِرْتَهَنُوهَا يَحُولُونَ بَيْنَهُ وَ بَيْنَهَا" قُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ قَدَرَ عَلَيْهَا خَالِياً وَ لَمْ يَعْلَمِ اَلَّذِينَ اِرْتَهَنُوهَا قَالَ "نَعَمْ لاَ أَرَى بِهَذَا بَأْساً".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4120 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who reported from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"I asked Imam (as) about a man who pledges his bondwoman (as collateral). Is it permissible for him to have intercourse with her?"
Imam (as) replied: "Those who have taken her as collateral (the creditors) prevent him from having access to her."
I then asked: "What if he manages to be with her privately without the knowledge of those who took her as collateral?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, I do not see any harm in this."
